replies.author: @justinj2170
replies.text: You just aren't read up on the Bible my friend... "In the beginning" God created animals and gave them a plant-based diet, with no indication or need for predation...: Genesis 1:29-30   Then God said,"I give you every seed bearing plant on the face of the earth and every tree that has fruit with seed in it. They will be yours for food. And to all the beasts of the earth and all the birds in the sky and all the creatures that move along the ground- everything that has a breath of life in it-I give green plants for food".  But then came 'the Fall' and 'the Flood', after which God permitted all humans to then eat meat. "The fear and dread of you will fall on all beasts of earth, birds in the sky, and fish in the sea, that are given into your hands.  Everything that lives and moves about will be food for you. Just as I gave you green plants, I now give you everything." The Bible promises that one day all carnivorous desires will stop and animals and humans will live in harmony. The Bible suggests predation isn't God's ultimate design, but a temporary aspect of the 'fallen order'.  And boy do I love good ribeye!
